summary: Full backend + frontend testing of the multi-vendor marketplace conversion. Backend: 46/46 pytest cases pass (19 legacy + 9 Stripe regression + 18 new multi-vendor cases). Seller registration returns role/status/store_name, duplicate email 400, pending seller blocked from /api/seller/products (403), admin approval flow works via ?status= query param, approved seller can CRUD only own products (edit/delete of official product returns 403), GET /api/seller/products returns only own, /api/seller/stats returns products/sales/revenue, GET /api/products includes seller products with correct seller_name and defaults seeded products to 'Nitro Nexus Official', admin-created products get 'Nitro Nexus Official', admin/stats includes sellers + pending_sellers. Legacy endpoints (products, orders, Stripe, PayPal shape) unaffected. Frontend: /seller shows register/login tabs with all data-testids, new registration lands on /seller/dashboard with pending-banner and hides List button, approved seller1@test.com sees stats + listings table, List product dialog creates a product visible in table AND public /products (with 'by Jay Gaming' attribution), navbar has 'Sell' link, product detail page shows 'Sold by {seller_name}'. Admin Sellers tab lists sellers with working approve/reject buttons; approving a pending seller removes their approve button (state updates via reload).

issues: Radix DialogContent renders console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby' — add DialogDescription for a11y (non-blocking).

critical_code_review_comments: server.py require_seller: admin tokens pass the check and would create products with seller_id=admin's user id (only intended for sellers). Admins should use /api/admin/products instead — consider restricting /api/seller/* to role=='seller' only, or explicitly stamping admin-created seller products with seller_name='Nitro Nexus Official'.
server.py list_sellers: no pagination — .to_list(500) hard cap could hide sellers at scale. Add limit/skip params.
server.py set_seller_status: silently succeeds even when seller_id does not match any doc (matched_count not checked). Return 404 if no seller was updated.
server.py PUT /admin/sellers/{id}/status: takes status via query param — REST convention prefers a JSON body. Works, but non-standard.
server.py seller_stats.revenue: iterates over all paid orders and matches by product_id in items — O(N*M). Acceptable for MVP but should be an aggregate pipeline once scale grows.
server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event(startup/shutdown) (carried over from prior iterations).
test_credentials: admin@nitronexus.gg / Admin@12345 ; seller1@test.com / Seller@123 (Jay Gaming, approved)
seed_data_creation: Registered several 'test_pending_/test_approve_/test_ui_' sellers and one 'TEST_UI_Listing_' product during testing (all left in DB, prefixed for future cleanup). One seller was approved by the UI test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-vendor conversion is fully working. When re-running tests, note that /admin/sellers accumulates TEST_/test_ sellers over runs; UI 'Sellers rows' count can drift. Emails are lowercased server-side — use lowercase in fixtures for exact matches. require_seller currently allows admin role through /seller/* endpoints (untested here). seller_id on products is stored as str(_id) — matches get_current_user's stringified _id.
rca of the issue: No functional bugs in this iteration. Only design/quality nits (a11y DialogDescription, unchecked matched_count on set_seller_status, deprecated on_event handlers).
